key_insight: A PINN-based, solver-agnostic training framework can learn turbulence closures that generalize across bluff-body shapes without embedding a CFD solver in the optimization loop.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more portable RANS closure development for aerodynamic and hydrodynamic design cases where geometry changes make traditional calibration brittle.
method: Train closures by enforcing the RANS residual with automatic differentiation inside a PINN.
Test four closure families: local stress map, non-local stress model with transported k, learned-length-scale stress model, and direct force model.
Deploy the learned closures frozen in a finite-element solver with input-gradient smoothing and a Lipschitz constraint for stability.
evidence_signal: The models were trained on six 2D bluff-body wakes at Re = 10^4 and evaluated in-sample and with leave-one-shape-out validation, where the force model achieved the best generalization and ~8.5% out-of-sample drag error.
watch_item: Performance depends on stability controls and frozen deployment, so solver robustness and extrapolation beyond the tested bluff-body family still need careful verification.
paper_title: Generalizable turbulence closures across bluff-body shapes by PINN-based solver-agnostic training
